seoTitle: Change Font Alignment in Power BI Single Value Cards
metaDescription: Learn how to change font alignment in Power BI single value cards to improve readability and give your dashboards a more polished look.
intro: Text alignment is a small formatting detail that quietly affects how easy a dashboard is to scan, especially on single value cards where the number is the whole point. This guide covers accessing the font settings for these cards and adjusting alignment for better visibility. It's a quick fix for report builders polishing dashboards before they go in front of stakeholders.

steps.heading: Access font settings
steps.body: Select the single value card visual and open its formatting pane to find the callout value or category label font options.

steps.heading: Change alignment options
steps.body: Adjust the text alignment setting — left, center, or right — to match how the card fits into your overall dashboard layout.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for alignment
steps.body: Center alignment tends to work well for standalone KPI cards, while left alignment fits better when cards sit beside labels or other text elements.

steps.heading: Avoid common formatting mistakes
steps.body: Watch for inconsistent alignment across a row of cards, which makes a dashboard look unfinished even if each individual card is formatted correctly.

faq.question: Where is the alignment setting for single value cards in Power BI?
faq.answer: It's found in the visual's formatting pane, typically under the Callout Value or General text settings depending on your Power BI version.

faq.question: Can I align the value and category label differently on the same card?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI generally lets you set alignment independently for the callout value and the category label within the same card visual.

faq.question: Why doesn't my alignment change appear to do anything?
faq.answer: This can happen if the card's text box is sized larger than the text itself, making alignment changes less visually obvious — try adjusting the card size to see the effect more clearly.

faq.question: Does alignment affect how the card looks on mobile Power BI views?
faq.answer: Yes, alignment carries over to mobile layouts, so it's worth checking how your cards look in the mobile view after making changes.
